response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile senior engineer, but their profile does not match this role's core requirements. Their C++ experience is limited to a short stint at National Instruments (~7 months in 2014–2015) and a thesis project, far short of the mandated 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. There is no SQL Server/Oracle experience indicated (MongoDB/MySQL instead), and no fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain exposure — RIDE Capital was a Node/TypeScript fintech product, not investment banking. Strong general engineering fundamentals cannot offset the absence of all three mandatory pillars.
strengths: 15+ years of broad professional software development experience across web, embedded, and desktop
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, trunk-based development, and open-source contributions
Systems-level and low-latency experience (DLang async I/O at Dunnhumby, some C/C++ exposure)
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months professionally, ended 2015), with no Windows-platform C++ depth
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain experience

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ experience vs. 5–10 year mandate
No fixed income / capital markets domain exposure
Strong general seniority and engineering craftsmanship
